Barbados (modern borders) — rum's birthplace
description: Modern political border (post-1966 independence). Rum first distilled here on British sugar plantations 1640s; built entirely on enslaved labour. Mount Gay (est. 1703) is the world's oldest rum brand.

Caribbean (modern borders) — core rum-producing region
description: Modern political borders. Jamaica, Cuba, Puerto Rico, Dominican Republic, Barbados, Trinidad — each island developed distinct rum styles from the same sugar-cane base. Industry grew out of slavery-era molasses economy.

Brazil (modern borders) — cachaça, legally distinct from rum
description: Modern political border. ~1.4 billion litres/year, distinct from rum by legal definition (cachaça is cane juice, not molasses). ~99% consumed domestically. Anchors the caipirinha cocktail.

USA / New England (modern borders) — historical triangle trade
description: Modern political border. 150+ Boston-area rum distilleries by 1750. New England rum was the American side of the triangle trade (rum → Africa → enslaved people → Caribbean). Industry collapsed after the 1807 Slave Trade Act.
